title: Cache Aware Serving
abstract: This paper studies prefix cache behavior for long document question answering.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Prefix reuse can reduce time to first token when several questions share the same long document. The cache benefit should be visible in warm runs.

sections.heading: Results
sections.text: The experiments show that cache hits lower prefill latency while random prefixes do not create the same reuse.

evidence: Prefix reuse can reduce time to first token

questions.question: What should random prefixes not create?

evidence: random prefixes do not create the same reuse

rights_note: Tiny fixture derived for tests; use upstream QASPER license for real data.

title: Long Context Evaluation
abstract: This paper studies long-context pressure and answer quality.

sections.heading: Method
sections.text: Needle retrieval tests place a known answer in a long context and ask the model to recover it.

sections.heading: Findings
sections.text: Quality checks should run with latency checks so a faster configuration does not hide incorrect answers.

questions.question: What do needle retrieval tests place in context?

evidence: place a known answer in a long context

questions.question: What should run with latency checks?

evidence: Quality checks should run with latency checks

rights_note: Tiny fixture derived for tests; use upstream QASPER license for real data.
